title: epic: jmap-chat crate API idiomatics cleanup (round 2)
description:
## Purpose

Address idiomatic Rust issues surfaced in the second post-implementation API review. These are API design and type-system issues -- not protocol correctness bugs -- that would draw comment in a code review from anyone familiar with Rust library conventions.

## Scope

All findings are in the jmap-chat library crate. No protocol spec changes required.

## Issues (priority order)

- stl1 (P1 bug): subscribe_events returns impl Stream -- not storable; should be BoxStream
- eyh8 (P2 bug): Id/UTCDate missing Borrow<str> -- HashMap queries require full Id construction
- 34mz (P2 bug): JmapRequestBuilder does not catch duplicate call IDs at build time
- t1wc (P2 bug): SessionClient lifetime borrows prevent storing in structs
- axzv (P3 bug): BearerAuth/BasicAuth do not derive Clone despite HeaderValue: Clone
- ijt0 (P3 bug): MessageQueryInput::sort_ascending lacks builder setter, inconsistent with peers
- zo1u (P3 bug): Id::new/UTCDate::new return ClientError -- domain type coupled to network error
- j1w7 (P4 bug): AuthProvider::auth_header couples trait to http::HeaderName/HeaderValue

## Design notes

The P1 fix (BoxStream) is a breaking API change but the correct long-term shape for a library stream. The P2 fixes (Borrow<str>, duplicate call ID detection, SessionClient owned) are also breaking but fix genuine ergonomics gaps. P3/P4 are non-breaking additions or internal cleanups.
close_reason: All 8 beads implemented, review loop complete, doc contract added
